content:
The Oxford 2024 employment study tracked 800+ occupations across 47 countries. Here's what the data actually shows about AI and your job:

REPLACED (automation above 70%):
• Data entry clerks
• Basic bookkeepers
• Telemarketing
• Credit analysts doing standard assessments
• Tax prep for simple returns

These jobs follow clear rules. AI excels at rules.

AUGMENTED (automation 30-50%):
• Radiologists (AI spots anomalies, doctors diagnose)
• Paralegals (AI does doc review, lawyers strategize)
• Software developers (AI writes boilerplate, humans architect)
• Graphic designers (AI generates options, humans direct)

These jobs got faster, not extinct. The humans doing them shifted up the value chain.

THE 3 IRREPLACEABLE SKILL CATEGORIES:

1. Complex problem-solving in novel situations
Real example: A manufacturing engineer solving why a production line fails only on humid Tuesdays. No pattern exists for AI to learn from.

2. Genuine human connection
Hospice nurses, therapists, executive coaches. The study found 0% automation potential for roles where trust and emotional reading matter more than information transfer.

3. Creative strategy under uncertainty
CMOs deciding to kill a winning product, researchers asking questions nobody thought to ask. AI optimizes known games. Humans change the game.

YOUR MOVE:

The data shows job losses concentrated in the "follow the manual" layer. Growth happening in the "judgment call" layer.

Retraining isn't about learning to code. It's about moving from executioner to decision-maker in your field.

Panic scrolls LinkedIn. Strategy opens the spreadsheet of what skills your industry actually pays for in 2024 vs 2019.

Which category are you in? And more importantly, which are you moving toward?
